summary: CC-0009 deploys OpenBao as a 3-replica HA Raft cluster via FluxCD HelmRelease with TLS, implements 5 idempotent bootstrap scripts (init-unseal, secret engines, auth, policies, bootstrap secrets), defines 8 HCL least-privilege policies matching the architecture doc exactly, wires a ClusterSecretStore connecting ESO to OpenBao, and creates 3 ExternalSecrets for credential materialization. The implementation is well-structured, follows established FluxCD and two-phase kustomization patterns, and includes comprehensive reference documentation. One blocking issue found: the mariadb-root-password ExternalSecret creates a K8s Secret key 'root-password' but the MariaDB CR expects key 'password', which will prevent MariaDB from reading its root password.

tests_checklist.item: Bootstrap scripts not tested (E2E tests deferred to CC-0010 per scope exclusion)
YAML manifests not structurally validated (shellcheck unavailable, E2E tests deferred to CC-0010)
No test infrastructure applicable — infrastructure-only feature with shell scripts and YAML manifests

code_quality_checklist.item: All bootstrap scripts use set -euo pipefail for strict error handling
All bootstrap scripts are idempotent with appropriate guard mechanisms
All HCL policies match architecture doc specifications exactly
HelmRelease values match canonical specification from architecture doc
Bootstrap scripts follow consistent internal structure (shebang, SPDX, config, helpers, main)
log() helper provides timestamped ISO 8601 output across all scripts
No dead code or commented-out code

security_checklist.item: No hardcoded secrets in any file
BAO_TOKEN passed via environment variable, not hardcoded
HCL policies enforce least-privilege access per consumer type
Generated passwords use cryptographically secure openssl rand -base64 32
write-bootstrap-secrets.sh never overwrites existing secrets (prevents accidental rotation)
Root token and unseal keys stored securely (K8s Secret by design; architecture doc recommends offline/HSM for production)

architecture_checklist.item: Follows FluxCD HelmRelease common install/upgrade settings pattern
Follows two-phase kustomization for CRD-dependent resources pattern
ESO resources in separate kustomization (third deployment phase after bootstrap)
ClusterSecretStore uses correct auth mount, role, and caProvider
Certificate SANs cover all StatefulSet pods and Service endpoint

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Scope matches plan exactly — excluded items (PushSecret, Transit auto-unseal, ServiceMonitor) not implemented
log() and kubectl exec wrapper functions duplicated across 5 scripts (acceptable for self-contained scripts)

fail_fast_checklist.item: All scripts exit immediately on command failure (set -e)
BAO_TOKEN validated with :? parameter expansion in scripts that need it
setup-policies.sh exits with error if no .hcl files found

defensive_checklist.item: init-unseal.sh checks bao status exit code before attempting initialization
setup-secret-engines.sh guards with bao secrets list before enabling
setup-auth.sh guards with bao auth list before enabling auth methods
write-bootstrap-secrets.sh guards with bao kv get before writing secrets
init-unseal.sh skips unseal for already-unsealed pods

security_findings.description: C2: ExternalSecret mariadb-root-password creates K8s Secret with key 'root-password' (secretKey: root-password) but the MariaDB CR at deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/mariadb.yaml:26 expects key 'password' (rootPasswordSecretKeyRef.key: password). MariaDB will fail to read its root password, breaking the database deployment. This is explicitly listed in the review criteria as a blocking acceptance criterion.
security_findings.fix: Change 'secretKey: root-password' to 'secretKey: password' on line 25. The remoteRef.property remains 'root-password' (the key name in OpenBao), but the secretKey (the key name in the created K8s Secret) must be 'password' to match what MariaDB expects.

security_findings.description: S5: Generated passwords in write-bootstrap-secrets.sh are passed as kubectl exec command-line arguments, making them transiently visible in process listings (/proc) on both the operator workstation and the pod. The shell expansion of 'password=$(generate_password)' happens before the kubectl exec call, so the cleartext password appears in the process argument list.
security_findings.fix: For production hardening, generate passwords inside the pod (kubectl exec ... -- openssl rand -base64 32) and pipe them to bao kv put via stdin. Acceptable as-is for initial bootstrap in non-production environments.

security_findings.description: S3/S5: init-unseal.sh stores the full bao operator init JSON output (containing root token and all 5 unseal keys) as a K8s Secret 'openbao-init-keys'. The architecture doc says 'CRITICAL: Store init-keys.json securely (offline, HSM, or split across operators)'. By-design per plan, but any K8s user with Secret read access in openbao-system namespace can retrieve the root token.
security_findings.fix: Document in the reference docs that the openbao-init-keys Secret should be exported and deleted after bootstrap for production deployments. Consider RBAC restrictions on Secret access in the openbao-system namespace.

architecture_findings.description: C1: ExternalSecret target Secret names deviate from the elaborated plan. Plan specifies 'keystone-admin-credentials' and 'keystone-db-credentials' as target K8s Secret names, but implementation uses 'keystone-admin' and 'keystone-db'. No downstream consumers exist yet, so this is not immediately breaking, but future Keystone deployment manifests will need to reference whichever name is chosen here. The implementation and reference docs are internally consistent with the shorter names.
architecture_findings.fix: Either (a) change target.name to 'keystone-admin-credentials' and 'keystone-db-credentials' to match the plan, or (b) explicitly document the naming deviation and update the plan to reflect the chosen names. Recommend option (a) for plan compliance.

architecture_findings.description: DA3: Reference documentation incorrectly describes MariaDB key mapping. The docs state: 'this key mapping is handled by the MariaDB CR definition (CC-0008), not the ExternalSecret.' This is factually incorrect — the MariaDB CRD's rootPasswordSecretKeyRef is a standard corev1.SecretKeySelector that looks for the exact key specified. There is no key remapping. The ExternalSecret MUST create a key named 'password' to match the MariaDB CR expectation.
architecture_findings.fix: After fixing the code bug (secretKey: root-password → password), update the docs note to accurately reflect the key mapping. The ExternalSecret remoteRef.property is 'root-password' (the OpenBao key name) and the secretKey is 'password' (the K8s Secret key name that MariaDB expects).

issues_found.description: ExternalSecret secretKey 'root-password' does not match MariaDB CR rootPasswordSecretKeyRef.key 'password'. The created K8s Secret will have key 'root-password' but MariaDB looks for key 'password'.
issues_found.fix: Change line 25 from 'secretKey: root-password' to 'secretKey: password'

issues_found.description: Target Secret name 'keystone-admin' deviates from plan specification 'keystone-admin-credentials'
issues_found.fix: Change target.name from 'keystone-admin' to 'keystone-admin-credentials', or update plan to reflect chosen name

issues_found.description: Target Secret name 'keystone-db' deviates from plan specification 'keystone-db-credentials'
issues_found.fix: Change target.name from 'keystone-db' to 'keystone-db-credentials', or update plan to reflect chosen name

issues_found.description: Documentation incorrectly claims MariaDB CR handles key mapping between 'root-password' and 'password'. The MariaDB CRD uses a standard SecretKeySelector with no remapping — it reads the exact key specified.
issues_found.fix: Update the note after fixing the ExternalSecret code bug. Document that secretKey maps OpenBao key 'root-password' to K8s Secret key 'password'

suggested_improvements: Extract shared shell functions (log, kubectl exec wrapper) into a common.sh sourced by all bootstrap scripts to reduce duplication across 5 files
Add a shellcheck CI check for deploy/openbao/bootstrap/*.sh scripts (shellcheck was unavailable in this review environment)
Consider adding a 'verify-bootstrap.sh' script that checks all expected secret engines, auth methods, policies, and secrets are in place — useful for operational validation after bootstrap
Document in the reference docs that the openbao-init-keys Secret should be exported to secure storage and deleted from the cluster after bootstrap for production deployments
next_steps: Update docs/reference/infrastructure/openbao-bootstrap.md ESO Integration table and MariaDB note to reflect correct key mapping after code fix
Decide on keystone ExternalSecret target names: align with plan ('keystone-admin-credentials', 'keystone-db-credentials') or update plan to match implementation ('keystone-admin', 'keystone-db')
Update docs/reference/infrastructure/openbao-bootstrap.md ESO Integration table if keystone target names change

metadata.title: D002: Implement OpenBao deployment and secret management
